meals.label: Meal A
meals.name: Ground Turkey Teriyaki Stir Fry
steps: Sauce: Combine ¾ cup water, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, honey, and brown sugar in a saucepan over medium. Bring to a low boil. Whisk remaining ¼ cup water with cornstarch; stir in slowly until thickened. Add orange zest. Set aside.
Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high. Sauté onion until softened. Add turkey; cook until fully cooked, 7–10 min.
Add broccoli and carrots; cook until tender.
Reduce heat to low; stir in teriyaki sauce. Simmer a few minutes. Serve over rice.
meals.note: 💡 Naturally not spicy and very kid-friendly. Steam the broccoli a couple minutes first if you want it softer for the boys.

meals.label: Meal B
meals.name: Marry Me Chicken Gnocchi
ings: 1¼ lb boneless skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 Tbsp olive oil (or oil from the sun-dried tomato jar)
1 small shallot or ½ onion, diced
⅔ cup sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed), drained and chopped
1 cup half-and-half (or ¾ cup heavy cream)
Red pepper flakes (optional, for adults only)
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken in a single layer 3–4 min per side until golden (doesn't need to be cooked through). Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and shallot to the skillet; sauté 2–3 min. Add garlic and sun-dried tomatoes; cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add uncooked gnocchi, breaking apart any stuck pieces. Pour in chicken broth and half-and-half; season with salt and pepper. Cover and cook over medium 5 min until gnocchi are tender and fluffy.
Return chicken to the pan. Add spinach; cook uncovered 3–5 min until chicken is cooked through and spinach is wilted.
Stir in Parmesan until melted and the sauce is creamy. Top with torn basil. Serve with red pepper flakes on the side for adults.
meals.note: 💡 The base recipe is mild — keep the red pepper flakes off to the side so the kids can eat it. Gnocchi cooks right in the sauce, no boiling needed.

meals.label: Meal C
meals.name: Sheet-Pan Greek Chicken & Veggies
ings: 1 medium zucchini, sliced into ½-inch half-moons
1 large red onion, cut into wedges
Cooked rice or warm pita, for serving
steps: Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon, garlic, oregano, thyme, salt, and pepper. Toss chicken with ⅔ of the marinade and let sit 15 min while the oven heats to 425°F.
Spread zucchini, bell pepper, red onion, and cherry tomatoes on a large sheet pan. Drizzle with the remaining marinade and toss to coat. Spread in a single layer.
Nestle the marinated chicken among the vegetables. Roast 25 min.
Remove pan, scatter crumbled feta over everything, and return to the oven 10–15 min more until chicken reaches 165°F and veggies are tender.
Spoon the pan juices over everything. Serve over rice or with warm pita.
meals.note: 💡 Naturally not spicy, pre-diabetic friendly. Pound thicker thighs to even thickness so they cook evenly.
